brief.title: aion-awl hygiene FINALE: purge the last lint bypasses (ast.rs 190 missing_docs by real docs, printer.rs vestigial missing_docs deletion, the three test files' expect_used/unwrap_used/format_push_string by Result-returning tests) AND wire `aion awl check` over tests/fixtures/*.awl in CI with a mechanically-visible bounded_cycle expected-failures carve-out pointing at AWL1-004, AND make the crate-wide zero-bypass grep a PERMANENT CI gate. crates/aion-awl/ goes zero-bypass and stays that way. Mechanically fenced scope.
brief.objective: Purge the last lint-bypass attributes in crates/aion-awl/ and wire the CI gates that keep the crate zero-bypass permanently, at ZERO behavior cost, touching ONLY six files (ast.rs, printer.rs, the three test files, .github/workflows/ci.yml). FOUR concerns, each mechanically gated. (1) ast.rs — delete the line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` and write REAL, informative `///` docs for all 190 missing-doc AST items (authoring-measured on base 3b22262a: 16 structs, 118 struct fields, 47 enum variants, 7 enums, 1 trait `Spanned`, 1 method `span`; docs-only, no signature change). Placeholder/identifier-restating docs (`The span.`, `The name.`) are a BLOCKING finding. (2) printer.rs — delete the line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` and CONFIRM clippy green; authoring measured this attr VESTIGIAL (printer.rs's only public item `pub fn print` already carries a doc, so deletion leaves clippy green with ZERO new errors). Do NO doc work and NO other edit to printer.rs — a one-line deletion, exactly like 001b's vestigial lib.rs attrs; leave `use crate::parser::duration_text;` (resolves through 001c's parser/mod.rs re-export). (3) The three test files (tests/awl2_defects.rs:8, tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs:16, tests/parser_printer.rs:3) — delete each `#![allow(...)]` and eliminate all 55 sites (awl2_defects 32, field_trivia 20, parser_printer 3) by REAL restructuring: `.expect(...)` on Result -> `#[test]` fn returns `Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>` + `?`; `.expect_err(...)` on Result -> `let Err(error) = parse(source) else { return Err("...".into()) };`; `.unwrap()`/`.expect(...)` on Option -> `.ok_or("...")?`; the single `format_push_string` (awl2_defects helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`) -> `use std::fmt::Write as _;` + `let _ = write!(src, ...)`. parser_printer.rs ALREADY returns Result and uses `?` — mirror it. NEVER re-add a bypass, `#[ignore]`, or a `.unwrap_or*` swallow; every test asserts the SAME behaviour. The crate has NO #[ignore]d and NO external-service tests, so the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` pattern does not arise. (4) CI — in .github/workflows/ci.yml (mirror the additive `codegen-drift`/`no-norn-in-platform` job convention) add an `awl-check` job running `aion awl check` over crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl that asserts the must-pass set {hello, research_report, research_report.canonical} exits 0 and holds the two bounded_cycle fixtures as an EXPLICIT expected failure that FAILS THE JOB if either ever exits 0, with an inline comment pointing at AWL1-004 (typed child contracts) as the brief that retires the carve-out; and add the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass grep gate (`! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/`). Do NOT wire the aspirational example docs approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl into this gate (they are AWL-1 documentation ahead of the implementation). After this brief crates/aion-awl/ carries ZERO bypasses and CI keeps it that way forever. The full R1-R6 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-input.json — read it in the workspace.
brief.context:
Fifth and FINAL of five one-concern sub-briefs decomposed from AWL0-REFAC-001 (see AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 and BRIEF-CRAFT.md entry 10 + generalised principle 5), stamped from the 001c/001d templates. 001a (checker split) merged adb6e4cf; 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) merged 80426594; 001c (parser split + allow-block purge) and 001d (emitter pure split) preceded this brief and landed the crate under the 500-line limit. This finale purges the LAST bypasses and makes the crate-wide zero-bypass state permanent. Its dispatch depends on BOTH 001c AND 001d having landed: at dispatch crates/aion-awl/src/parser/ and src/emitter/ folder modules exist (parser.rs/emitter.rs gone), 001c already purged parser.rs's line-1 block, and emitter carried no block. This brief's six targets are protected from 001c/001d by their scope fences, so the authoring measurements below (base 3b22262ad308a44c4fa86cc0c452d41b8b3c3633 = current main) are EXACT at dispatch.

THIS BRIEF IS PART PURGE-BY-REAL-WORK (ast.rs docs + the three test files) AND PART VESTIGIAL-DELETION (printer.rs) AND PART CI-WIRING. Unlike the single-file splits, it is MULTI-CONCERN BY DESIGN (the finale) — see the SIZING note for the authoring recommendation to consider splitting at dispatch.

INTENTION: The monolithic AWL0-REFAC-001 failed twice — once by fraud (include! splices + crate-wide allow), once by an honest rollback after cascading errors mid-split of four files at once — so it was decomposed smallest-first. Every non-negotiable here is a mechanical gate (four GREEN on base: scoped-companion, lints-pinned, scope-fence, awl-check-contract; two RED on base BY DESIGN: crate-wide-zero-bypass and ci-wired, because they ARE the work). The scope fence makes wandering impossible, and the full measured inventory is embedded so the developer neither hunts phantom purge work nor improvises the plan-vs-reality divergences.

CONSTRAINTS:
- CN1: Canonical docs are the reading list; the plan's Wave 0 AWL0-REFAC-001e entry is the CONTRACT — implement it verbatim. Three plan-vs-reality divergences were resolved at authoring (see notes DIVERGENCES); discovered ambiguities are findings to report, never license to improvise.
- CN2: Coding standards (CLAUDE.md): no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses, thiserror in libraries, no unwrap()/expect()/panic!() in library code, tests may return Result and use ?, files <=500 lines, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, no silent failures. #[ignore] is REJECTED; the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` runtime-skip is the only sanctioned alternative for external-service tests — this crate has none, so it does not arise.
- CN3: Gate argv, run EXACTLY in order before reporting: mechanical gates (no-bypasses-scoped-companion, no-bypasses-crate-wide, lints-pinned, scope-fence, ci-wired, awl-check-contract) -> cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli.
- CN4: Round-trip property is sacred (parse . print = id, byte-level, every fixture) and the emitter .gleam.golden files are byte-pinned; this brief must not perturb any of it (the scope fence and the docs-only/one-line-deletion edits make perturbing it impossible).
- CN5: Review lenses: the standard adversarial set PLUS a spec-fidelity lens — clause-by-clause against R1-R6, including the docs-quality clause (placeholder ast.rs docs are BLOCKING), that printer.rs was a vestigial delete-and-confirm, that every test's asserted behaviour is preserved with no swallow/ignore/re-added bypass, and that the bounded_cycle carve-out is an EXPLICIT mechanically-visible expected-failure pointing at AWL1-004 (a silent skip or a fixture edited/deleted to pass is BLOCKING).
- CN6: Bar reminders: stage explicit paths (never `git add -A`); inspect with `git diff --no-ext-diff`; NEVER trust the workflow's own green — the operator re-runs the gates by hand before merge.

The full R1-R6 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-input.json — read it in the workspace.
pointers: docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001c-input.json (the purge-by-doing-the-work template — docs written, unwrap/expect eliminated)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001d-input.json (the latest corrected gate forms; the pure-split/purge distinction; the scoped-companion no-bypasses gate this brief reuses)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001b-input.json (the vestigial-attr delete-and-confirm precedent — printer.rs here is the same shape)

docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md (Wave 0 — the AWL0-REFAC-001e entry: the CONTRACT; note it says `ast.rs + printer.rs missing_docs purge` and `EXCEPT the two bounded_cycle fixtures ... pointing at AWL1-004`)

docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/BRIEF-CRAFT.md (why the gates are shaped this way; entries 8-12, generalised principles 1-12, principle 10 MEASURE-before-mandating, and the run a96973cb / 3d201c36 validation notes)
crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs (delete line-1 attr; write 190 real docs — the whole AWL AST type surface)
crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs (delete line-1 attr; VESTIGIAL — confirm clippy green, no doc work, one-line diff; leave the duration_text import)
crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs, tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs, tests/parser_printer.rs (delete each attr; convert to Result-returning tests — parser_printer.rs already shows the shape)
.github/workflows/ci.yml (add the awl-check fixtures job + the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass gate; mirror the additive `codegen-drift`/`no-norn-in-platform` jobs)
crates/aion-cli/src/awl.rs (the `aion awl check` subcommand — `check <file>` parses+typechecks one .awl, exits non-zero + prints a diagnostic on error, `ok: <file> (N steps)` on success)

crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/ (the five committed checker fixtures: hello.awl, research_report.awl, research_report.canonical.awl PASS; bounded_cycle.awl, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl FAIL = the carve-out)
crates/aion-awl/src/checker/ and crates/aion-awl/src/lexer/ (the landed folder modules — DO NOT EDIT; the scoped-companion gate guards them)
scope_in: crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs (delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`; write real docs for all 190 items; no signature change)
crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs (delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`; confirm clippy green — vestigial, one-line diff, no other edit)
crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs (delete line-8 attr; convert 32 sites to Result-returning tests)
crates/aion-awl/tests/parser_printer.rs (delete line-3 attr; convert 3 sites; the file already uses Result+?)
.github/workflows/ci.yml (add the awl-check fixtures job with the bounded_cycle expected-failures carve-out pointing at AWL1-004, and the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass grep gate)
scope_out: REAL DOCS, NOT PLACEHOLDERS (R1): ast.rs's 190 doc comments must be informative sentences; `The span.`, restating an identifier, or any placeholder is a BLOCKING finding. Docs only — no signature change.

printer.rs IS DELETE-AND-CONFIRM (R2): the attr is vestigial (measured — clippy green after deletion, one already-documented public fn). Delete the single line, confirm clippy green, make NO other edit; do NOT touch `use crate::parser::duration_text;`.

TESTS RETURN Result, NO SWALLOW, NO #[ignore] (R3): eliminate all 55 sites by ?/ok_or/let-else/write!. NEVER re-add a bypass, NEVER #[ignore], NEVER `.unwrap_or*`-swallow. Every test asserts the SAME behaviour. No external-service tests exist, so no env-gate is in play.

CI CARVE-OUT MUST BE MECHANICALLY VISIBLE (R4): the two bounded_cycle fixtures are an EXPECTED failure that fails the job if either ever checks clean, with an inline AWL1-004 pointer. A silent skip, a deleted fixture, or a fixture edited to pass are each BLOCKING. Do NOT wire approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl (aspirational AWL-1 docs) into this gate.

NO LINT-POLICY WEAKENING ANYWHERE (R5): no bypass introduced in src or tests; crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml byte-identical (dispatch 2 of the parent brief was rejected for a private manifest allow-list). The crate-wide zero-bypass grep must be GREEN and wired permanently in CI.

SCOPE FENCE (R6): the diff touches ONLY the six owned paths. lib.rs, parser/, emitter/, checker/, lexer/, Cargo.toml, fixtures, goldens, canonicals, every other crate are UNMODIFIED. If you believe an edit outside the fence is required, STOP and report it as a finding.
ZERO behavior change: no AST shape/span/diagnostic/emitted-Gleam/verdict change; the round-trip property and emitter goldens are untouched.
SHALL NOT touch any crate other than aion-awl (except the shared .github/workflows/ci.yml, which this brief owns), nor anything in aion-awl outside the six owned paths.
acceptance: no-bypasses-scoped-companion gate (GREEN on base): grep over checker/, lib.rs, lexer/ plus parser/ and emitter/ once present returns no allow/expect/ignore/include! matches — it scans only EXISTING directories (the `[ -e ]` guards match parser/ and emitter/ the DIRECTORIES), never this brief's targets, so it is green on base and protects the landed splits.

no-bypasses-CRATE-WIDE gate (RED on base BY DESIGN — the headline): `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` — red on base (targets still carry bypasses), green only when every one is purged.
lints-pinned gate (GREEN on base): crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml has exactly one [lints] section, containing workspace = true.
scope-fence gate: git diff versus the pinned base SHA, excluding the six owned paths, is empty.

ci-wired gate (RED on base BY DESIGN): ci.yml wires the `aion awl check` fixtures job, the AWL1-004 carve-out pointer, and the crate-wide bypass-pattern permanent gate — red on base, green when all three are wired.

awl-check-contract gate (behavioural guard, GREEN on base): builds aion, runs check over tests/fixtures/*.awl, asserts must-pass set exits 0 and the two bounded_cycle fixtures exit non-zero (fails if a bounded_cycle fixture is edited to pass — guards against silent green).

cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli, all clean. (The full workspace suite is run by the operator at merge review while tasks #243/#244 are open.)

Hand review: ast.rs docs are REAL sentences (no placeholders) with no signature change; printer.rs is a one-line deletion, clippy green; the three test files return Result with every asserted behaviour intact and no swallow/ignore/re-added bypass; ci.yml's awl-check job has the mechanically-visible bounded_cycle expected-failure carve-out pointing at AWL1-004 (not a silent skip), the aspirational examples excluded; the crate-wide zero-bypass grep is wired permanently; Cargo.toml/lib.rs/parser/emitter/checker/lexer/fixtures/goldens untouched; the crate-wide no-bypasses grep is GREEN.
brief.notes:
DISPATCH HISTORY (read this first): the parent brief AWL0-REFAC-001 was dispatched twice and REJECTED twice at operator review, then decomposed into five one-concern sub-briefs. Dispatch 1 faked the split with include!("parts/part_00.rs") textual splices behind 2-line mod.rs files plus a crate-wide #![allow(...)]. Dispatch 2 rolled the split back after cascading errors and instead replaced aion-awl's Cargo.toml `[lints] workspace = true` with a private allow-list to silence clippy. BOTH escape hatches are now mechanical gates that run before anything expensive; do the real work. The pilots then landed FIRST-PASS clean: 001a (checker split) merged adb6e4cf, 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) merged 80426594 (8/8 gates, 4/4 lenses, zero deviations each); 001c (parser split + allow-block purge) and 001d (emitter pure split) preceded this brief and landed the crate under the 500-line limit. This finale is stamped from them. Honest reporting was noted favourably every time: if something genuinely cannot be done within the boundaries, say so plainly in your report rather than working around a gate.

THIS IS THE HYGIENE FINALE — part real-work purge, part vestigial deletion, part CI wiring (measure-before-mandating, BRIEF-CRAFT principle 10). It owns the LAST bypasses in the crate. Do NOT hunt for purge work that measurement says does not exist (printer.rs), and do NOT introduce gratuitous edits under cover of the purge.

TECHNIQUE: (1) ast.rs — delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`, write real `///` docs for all 190 items (the whole AWL AST type surface). Describe what each type/field/variant IS or LOCATES; for `pub span: Span` say what it spans, for `pub name: String` say what it identifies. Some fields already carry docs (Document::finish_leading/finish_trailing/epilogue_comments; the leading_comments/trailing_comments on ActionDecl/StepDecl/HandlerBlock) — keep or improve them, never regress. No signature change. (2) printer.rs — delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`, run clippy, confirm green. It is VESTIGIAL: printer.rs's only public item `pub fn print(document: &Document) -> String` (line 11) already carries `/// Render an AWL document in the canonical AWL-0 format.`. This is a one-line diff, exactly like 001b's four vestigial lib.rs attrs — no doc-writing, no other edit; leave the `use crate::parser::duration_text;` import (it resolves through 001c's parser/mod.rs re-export). (3) The three test files — delete each attr, convert to Result-returning tests. parser_printer.rs ALREADY returns `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>` and uses `?` in most tests — mirror that shape. Site classes and fixes: `.expect(...)` on Result -> return Result + `?`; `.expect_err(...)` on Result (assert parse FAILS) -> `let Err(error) = parse(source) else { return Err("<why>".into()) };` then inspect `error`; `.unwrap()`/`.expect(...)` on Option (`.find(...)`, `.nth(1).map(...)`, `.as_ref()`, a `match{...}` -> Option) -> `.ok_or("<what was missing>")?`; the single format_push_string (awl2_defects helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`, `src.push_str(&format!(...))`) -> `use std::fmt::Write as _;` + `let _ = write!(src, ...)`. NEVER `.unwrap_or*`-swallow, NEVER re-add a bypass, NEVER `#[ignore]`. (4) CI — add an additive fast-lane job to .github/workflows/ci.yml (mirror `codegen-drift`: build/run the aion binary; and `no-norn-in-platform`: a mechanical assertion job). The awl-check job runs `aion awl check` over each crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl, asserts {hello, research_report, research_report.canonical} exit 0, and holds {bounded_cycle.awl, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl} as an EXPECTED failure that FAILS THE JOB if either exits 0, with an inline comment naming AWL1-004. Also add the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass grep gate. Do NOT wire approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl (aspirational AWL-1 docs).

MEASURED INVENTORY (authoring, on base 3b22262ad308a44c4fa86cc0c452d41b8b3c3633 = current main, `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings`). FULL crate bypass grep (`grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/`) returns SIX sites: crates/aion-awl/src/parser.rs:1 `#![allow(missing_docs, clippy::expect_used, clippy::too_many_lines, clippy::unnecessary_wraps, clippy::unwrap_used)]` (OWNED BY 001c — GONE at dispatch, parser/ folder exists); crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs:1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` (THIS BRIEF); crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs:1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` (THIS BRIEF — VESTIGIAL); crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs:8 `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::format_push_string)]` (THIS BRIEF); crates/aion-awl/tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs:16 `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::format_push_string)]` (THIS BRIEF); crates/aion-awl/tests/parser_printer.rs:3 `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::unwrap_used)]` (THIS BRIEF). There is NO `#[ignore]` and NO `include!(` anywhere in the crate; emitter.rs carries no block (001d). PER-TARGET FALLOUT: (a) ast.rs block deleted -> exactly 190 missing_docs errors, ALL in ast.rs, NO other lint: 16 structs, 118 struct fields, 47 enum variants, 7 enums, 1 trait (`Spanned`), 1 method (`span`). Docs-only; no unwrap/expect/too_many_lines/unnecessary_wraps; no public-fn signature pressure. (b) printer.rs block deleted -> ZERO clippy errors (VESTIGIAL): printer.rs has exactly ONE public item, `pub fn print` (line 11), already documented — clippy stays GREEN. Delete-and-confirm, no doc work. (c) three test files' attrs deleted -> 55 sites: awl2_defects.rs 32, field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs 20, parser_printer.rs 3. Lint composition crate-wide: 16 `expect_err()` on Result, 16 `expect()` on Result, 10 `expect()` on Option, 11 `unwrap()` on Option, 1 `unwrap()` on Result, 1 `format_push_string` (awl2_defects.rs helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`). ALL are production test paths. field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs's attr lists `clippy::format_push_string` but that file has NO such site (that portion of its allow is vestigial); deleting the whole line is still correct. The crate has NO #[ignore]d tests and NO external-service tests, so the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` pattern does not arise. CI RECON: CI lives in .github/workflows/ci.yml (fast lane: fmt / clippy / no-norn-in-platform / standalone-crates / test / codegen-drift). `aion awl check <file>` (crates/aion-cli/src/awl.rs) parses+typechecks ONE .awl, prints `ok: <file> (N steps)` + exit 0 on success, a `<file>:<line>:<col>: error: <message>` diagnostic + non-zero exit on failure. Authoring ran `aion awl check` over all nine committed .awl: crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/hello.awl ok(2), research_report.awl ok(7), research_report.canonical.awl ok(7); bounded_cycle.awl FAILS `crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/bounded_cycle.awl:22:3: error: as binding `state` expected ReviewState, found untyped child result`; bounded_cycle.canonical.awl FAILS `...bounded_cycle.canonical.awl:23:3: error: as binding `state` expected ReviewState, found untyped child result`; docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/examples/dev_brief.awl ok(8); approval_gate.awl FAILS `...:60:3: error: unknown step field `otherwise``; sized_fanout.awl FAILS `...:19:1: error: type declaration needs record fields`; examples/awl-hello/awl_hello.awl ok(2).

DIVERGENCES (plan vs measured reality — resolved at authoring, stated as findings per CN1): (1) The plan says `ast.rs + printer.rs missing_docs purge`, implying BOTH need doc-writing. MEASURED: printer.rs's attr is VESTIGIAL (delete-and-confirm, zero docs) while ast.rs is 190 real doc items. Resolved: R1 = ast.rs real-docs purge; R2 = printer.rs vestigial deletion (like 001b). (2) The plan does not quantify ast.rs; it is 190 items (the whole AST surface) — embedded as the R1 inventory so the developer starts fully informed. (3) The plan says wire `aion awl check` over `every committed .awl fixture ... EXCEPT the two bounded_cycle fixtures`. MEASURED: `aion awl check` over EVERY committed .awl yields FOUR failures — the two bounded_cycle fixtures AND the two aspirational AWL-1 EXAMPLE docs (approval_gate.awl = `otherwise`/AWL1-005, sized_fanout.awl = enum decl/AWL1-002). Resolved: the gate is scoped to the CHECKER FIXTURE set crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl (five files, of which exactly the two bounded_cycle fixtures fail), which makes the plan's `two bounded_cycle fixtures` carve-out EXACTLY complete and matches the gate's stated purpose (`closes the blind spot that hid the bounded_cycle checker failures`). The two aspirational example docs live under docs/design/.../examples/ (labeled `AWL-1, aspirational` in the plan's canonical-docs list), are documentation ahead of the implementation, and are OUT OF this checker-fixtures gate's scope — not carve-outs. A future AWL-1 brief may add an examples-check gate; that is out of 001e's contract.

SIZING (authoring judgment — the operator decides): this finale is by a WIDE margin the heaviest of the five sub-briefs and spans THREE distinct skills — 190 AST doc sentences under a no-placeholder bar (R1), 55 test-site conversions across three files (R3), and a novel CI mechanism with a self-retiring expected-failure carve-out (R4/R5). 001c (the previously-heaviest split) was 32 clippy errors collapsing to a handful of patterns; 001e's ast.rs docs ALONE are an order of magnitude more volume, and the CI wiring is a context-switch into a different surface. In authoring's honest judgment this EXCEEDS the one-dev-round + 3-fix-cycle envelope that BRIEF-CRAFT entry 10 / principle 5 warns against, and authoring RECOMMENDS the operator SPLIT it at dispatch into: 001e-a HYGIENE PURGE (ast.rs docs + printer.rs vestigial deletion + the three test files) — this makes crates/aion-awl/ zero-bypass and its crate-wide gate is the red-on-base completion signal; and 001e-b CI WIRING (the awl-check fixtures job + the AWL1-004 carve-out + the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass CI gate) — a tiny diff scoped to .github/workflows/ci.yml, DEPENDENT on 001e-a landing (the permanent gate can only go green after the purge). The brief is authored WHOLE and is dispatchable whole (all six gates and R1-R6 are internally consistent for a single dispatch); the split is a right-sizing safeguard, not a correctness requirement. If dispatched whole and a fix cycle is consumed by the ast.rs docs, report an honest partial rather than a rushed one — the operator would rather redispatch (or carve) than merge placeholder docs or a broken carve-out.

GATES: all six mechanical gates were verified on the base ref at authoring. FOUR are GREEN ON BASE (any red in them is yours): no-bypasses-scoped-companion (exit 0 — it scans checker/, lib.rs, lexer/, and parser/ + emitter/ only if the DIRECTORIES exist; on this base parser/ and emitter/ do NOT yet exist so parser.rs's line-1 block is NOT scanned and the gate stays green — verified; it went RED on a synthetic bypass planted in the landed lexer/, proving it guards landed work), lints-pinned (exit 0), scope-fence (exit 0 on the clean tree; correctly exit 1 on a synthetic out-of-scope lib.rs edit; exit 0 on a synthetic in-scope ast.rs edit), and awl-check-contract (exit 0 — must-pass fixtures pass, bounded_cycle fixtures fail-as-expected). TWO are RED ON BASE BY DESIGN because they ARE the work: no-bypasses-CRATE-WIDE (exit 1 on base — ast.rs/printer.rs/the three test files still carry bypasses; verified it FLIPS to exit 0 when all bypass lines are synthetically removed), and ci-wired (exit 1 on base — ci.yml has none of the `awl check` / `AWL1-004` / crate-wide-grep wiring yet; green when all three are wired). The scope-fence gate is pinned to a base SHA: the literal string PINNED_SHA_AT_DISPATCH in the scope-fence argv MUST be replaced by the operator with `git rev-parse main` at dispatch time — 001c AND 001d will have merged since authoring, so main WILL have moved and the pin MUST be refreshed; the operator re-verifies the four green-on-base gates green + the two red-on-base gates red on that fresh base before dispatching. The test gates are scoped (aion-awl + aion-cli) and deterministic — the flaky workspace suite is deliberately NOT a gate here (tasks #243/#244: aion-package `append_run_regenerates_type_checking_gleam` and aion-server --lib both flake under load in crates this diff never touches); if a workspace test outside aion-awl/aion-cli ever runs and flakes while every in-scope gate passes and your diff touches only the six owned paths, re-run once and report the flake honestly rather than attempting an out-of-scope fix. The operator runs the full workspace suite by hand at merge review. AUTHORING VERIFICATION (clean detached worktree on base 3b22262ad308a44c4fa86cc0c452d41b8b3c3633, PINNED_SHA substituted to that SHA): no-bypasses-scoped-companion exit 0 (parser.rs's line-1 block present and unscanned; RED when a synthetic bypass was planted in lexer/), no-bypasses-crate-wide exit 1 (RED on base; exit 0 with all six bypass lines synthetically deleted), lints-pinned exit 0, scope-fence exit 0 clean (exit 1 on a synthetic out-of-scope lib.rs edit; exit 0 on a synthetic in-scope ast.rs edit), ci-wired exit 1 (RED on base), awl-check-contract exit 0 (must-pass fixtures pass, bounded_cycle fixtures fail-as-expected). Base `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` was GREEN on the unmodified base (all blocks present).

gates.name: no-bypasses-scoped-companion
argv: ps="crates/aion-awl/src/checker crates/aion-awl/src/lib.rs crates/aion-awl/src/lexer"; [ -e crates/aion-awl/src/parser ] && ps="$ps crates/aion-awl/src/parser"; [ -e crates/aion-awl/src/emitter ] && ps="$ps crates/aion-awl/src/emitter"; ! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' $ps

gates.name: lints-pinned
argv: test "$(grep -c '^\[lints' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml)" -eq 1 && grep -A1 '^\[lints\]$' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml | grep -q '^workspace = true'

gates.name: awl-check-contract
argv: cargo build -p aion-cli --bin aion -q && B=target/debug/aion; s=0; for f in crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/hello.awl crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/research_report.awl crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/research_report.canonical.awl; do "$B" awl check "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "MUST-PASS FAILED: $f"; s=1; }; done; for f in crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/bounded_cycle.awl crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/bounded_cycle.canonical.awl; do "$B" awl check "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { echo "EXPECTED-FAIL NOW GREEN (AWL1-004 landed - retire carve-out): $f"; s=1; }; done; exit $s

lenses.name: correctness
lenses.charter: Hunt defects in the change itself: logic errors, unhandled cases, broken invariants, silent failure paths. The highest-value defect classes here: (1) a test-side unwrap/expect elimination that changed which branch runs or which case is asserted — an `expect_err` converted to a let-else that no longer proves parse FAILED, an `.ok_or(...)?` whose error path is now reachable when the value was actually always present, a `?` that short-circuits a test before its assertions; (2) an ast.rs doc that is subtly WRONG about what a field means (a doc is a claim — verify it against the field's use in parser/printer/checker); (3) a CI carve-out that does not actually fail when a bounded_cycle fixture starts passing (the guard-against-silent-green must be real). Assume the change is wrong and construct the input or state that proves it.

lenses.name: regressions
lenses.charter: Hunt collateral damage: behaviour the diff changes that the brief did not ask to change. Every test must assert the SAME thing it did on base_ref — same source parses/fails identically, same span/line/column/message assertions hold; a test that now passes for a different reason (or no longer exercises the failure it was written to guard) is a regression. The round-trip property (parse . print = id) and the emitter .gleam.golden files must be untouched (the scope fence forbids editing fixtures/goldens, but verify no ast.rs/printer.rs edit perturbed emitted output). Watch for a gratuitous edit smuggled under the purge (a printer.rs change beyond the one-line attr deletion, an ast.rs signature/field change, a test whose assertions were weakened to make the ?-conversion compile). Any fixture/golden/canonical edit is an automatic regression.

lenses.name: brief_compliance
lenses.charter: Hold the diff to the brief, adversarially: every acceptance criterion either demonstrably met or a blocking finding; every scope_out boundary respected; every claimed deviation actually declared. Pass-and-still-wrong is the failure class — do not accept a claim without evidence in the diff or gate output. Confirm specifically: R1 ast.rs docs are REAL (spot-check for placeholders — `The span.`, restating an identifier — which are BLOCKING) and no signature changed; R2 printer.rs is a ONE-LINE deletion with clippy green and no other edit; R3 zero unwrap/expect/expect_err remain in the three test files with no `.unwrap_or*` swallow, no `#[ignore]`, no re-added bypass; R4 the bounded_cycle carve-out FAILS the job if a fixture checks clean (not a silent skip) and the aspirational examples are NOT wired; R5 the crate-wide grep is green and permanently wired and Cargo.toml is byte-identical; R6 only the six owned paths changed.

lenses.name: spec_fidelity
lenses.charter: Read AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 (the AWL0-REFAC-001e entry — `ast.rs + printer.rs missing_docs purge, the three test files' expect_used/unwrap_used/format_push_string purge (tests return Result + ?), wire aion awl check over every committed .awl fixture in CI ... EXCEPT the two bounded_cycle fixtures ... pointing at AWL1-004 ... the crate-wide zero-bypass gate goes green here and stays a permanent gate`) and the committed brief docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-input.json, and check the implementation clause by clause against R1-R6. (R1) ast.rs's line-1 allow gone and all 190 items carry REAL informative docs — placeholder/uninformative docs (`The span.`, restating the identifier) are a BLOCKING finding; no signature change. (R2) printer.rs's line-1 allow gone via a delete-and-confirm — the plan implied a doc purge but authoring measured it VESTIGIAL (clippy green after deletion, one already-documented public fn), so verify NO gratuitous edit and NO doc churn was smuggled in. (R3) the three test files carry zero bypass attrs and zero unwrap/expect/expect_err, converted to Result-returning tests via ?/ok_or/let-else/write! with every asserted behaviour preserved — no swallow, no #[ignore], no re-added bypass, no invented behaviour. (R4) `aion awl check` is wired over crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl with the two bounded_cycle fixtures held as an EXPLICIT, mechanically-visible expected-failure that FAILS THE JOB if either checks clean, pointing at AWL1-004 — a silent skip, a deleted fixture, or a fixture edited to pass are BLOCKING; the aspirational examples approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl are correctly EXCLUDED (they are AWL-1 documentation ahead of the implementation — verify they were not wired and not edited). (R5) the crate-wide zero-bypass grep is GREEN and wired as a PERMANENT CI gate; crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml [lints] is byte-identical (workspace = true only); clippy -D warnings green. (R6) the diff touches ONLY the six owned paths. ANY lint-policy weakening (source attr OR manifest OR a relocated bypass), any placeholder doc, any unwrap/expect left or swallowed, any #[ignore], any fixture/golden edit, any silent carve-out, or any change outside the fence is an automatic blocking finding — both prior dispatches of the parent brief were rejected for the fraud patterns.
